denarii.name: denarii
denarii.description: The silver coin of the Aquilan Imperium, stamped with the Old Raven's profile. The common currency of trade, bribe, and tribute across the provinces.

Bronze Charter-Tag.name: Bronze Charter-Tag
Bronze Charter-Tag.description: A plain disc of stamped bronze on a leather cord, struck with the iron-grey sigil of the Free Companies and a registry number. Worn openly at the throat it marks its bearer a D-Rank companion of the guild; slipped out of sight it leaves them an ordinary traveler. The lowest rung of the one ladder birth cannot bar.

Silver Charter-Tag.name: Silver Charter-Tag
Silver Charter-Tag.description: A silver disc on a worn cord, the Companies' sigil and a registry number struck clean across its face. Displayed at the throat it names a C-Rank companion, trusted to range and work alone anywhere the charter runs; pocketed, it draws none of that notice. Real standing, earned in the field.

Steel Charter-Tag.name: Steel Charter-Tag
Steel Charter-Tag.description: A blackened steel disc on a braided cord, the guild seal struck deep enough to last a hard career. Worn plainly it marks a B-Rank companion, a proven professional whose name travels the contract boards ahead of them; concealed, it lets them pass unremarked. Few wear steel, and fewer still earn it from a desk.

Gold Charter-Tag.name: Gold Charter-Tag
Gold Charter-Tag.description: A disc of struck gold bearing the Companies' seal and the bearer's name, hung on a fine cord. Displayed, it names an A-Rank companion whose arrival quietly rearranges a room and opens doors that rank alone cannot; out of sight, it leaves them anyone at all. The highest emblem the guild grants from service.

Companion's Blade.name: Companion's Blade
Companion's Blade.description: The Free Companies' standard-issue sword: a plain, well-balanced arming blade with an iron-grey cord wound at the grip and the guild's small sigil stamped at the ricasso. No flourish, no maker's vanity -- only reliable steel, drawn from the chapterhouse armory and meant to be used hard and kept sharp.

Free Company Travelling Leathers.name: Free Company Travelling Leathers
Free Company Travelling Leathers.description: A hard-wearing jerkin of boiled leather reinforced with riveted strips, cut for the long road and the longer contract. Guild-issue and unglamorous, dyed the Companies' iron-grey, it turns a glancing blow and shrugs off weather from the northern limes to the southern coast.

Road-Warden's Cloak.name: Road-Warden's Cloak
Road-Warden's Cloak.description: A heavy travelling cloak of oiled wool in Company grey, weighted at the hem and clasped with a plain iron brooch. It sheds rain and road-dust, doubles as a bedroll on a cold night, and its weighted folds have turned more than one knife meant for a companion's back.

Contract-Satchel.name: Contract-Satchel
Contract-Satchel.description: A road-worn satchel of stiff leather divided into waxed compartments, made to carry sealed contracts, a seal-matrix, a tally of denarii, and the chits that prove a job done and reported. Every working companion carries one; an empty satchel marks a companion between contracts, a fat one a good season.

Muster Wine.name: Muster Wine
Muster Wine.description: A stone jug of the rough, strong barley-wine the Mercenary Muster is known for, traded by the cup across every chapterhouse. It warms the road out of a companion's bones and, two cups in, loosens the road-news out of their mouth.
